interviewStages: Behavioral: ownership, customer empathy for lawyers, regulated-industry care
codingTopics: NLP and text: chunking, tokenization, retrieval over documents
Search and ranking: vector plus keyword retrieval, prefix search
Pipelines: RAG ingestion and evaluation harnesses
Data structures: inverted indexes, tries, heaps for top-K
systemDesignTopics: Design a RAG pipeline over millions of legal documents per customer
Design a contract-analysis system that compares thousands of provisions
Design an evaluation harness for legal-correctness with attorney review
Design tenant-isolated retrieval that keeps privileged client data separate

coding: Chunk a long legal document into overlapping passages respecting clause boundaries. Discuss the sliding-window approach and tradeoffs.
Given a query and an inverted index over contract clauses, return the top-K most relevant clauses using a heap. Discuss ranking.
Build a citation-grounding checker that verifies each sentence in an LLM answer maps to a retrieved source passage.
Deduplicate near-identical clauses across a document set using shingling and hashing. Walk through complexity.
behavioral: Harvey serves lawyers in a high-stakes domain. Tell me about a time correctness and care mattered more than speed.
Describe a time you took ownership of an ambiguous AI or evaluation problem end to end.
Give an example of building empathy for a non-technical expert user like an attorney.
Tell me about a time an LLM or ML system produced a wrong output and how you built guardrails.
systemDesign: Design a RAG pipeline over millions of legal documents, isolated per customer.
Design a contract-analysis system that compares thousands of provisions across agreements.
Design an evaluation harness that measures legal-correctness with attorney review in the loop.
values: Harvey works with privileged legal data. Describe a time you handled sensitive data with extra care.
How do you evaluate an AI feature for a domain where a wrong answer has real consequences?
Tell me about a time you earned the trust of a skeptical expert user.
other: How does Harvey compare to Casetext or EvenUp?

rawSummary:
Harvey Interview Guide (2026): Legal AI Platform
By 			
				
				techinterview.org			
			
		

		 /  May 6, 2026 
			
		

	
	
	

		
		
On this page

- Process

- What they actually ask

- Levels and comp (2026)

- Prep priorities

- Frequently Asked Questions

Updated May 6, 2026 · techinterview.org

⏱ 1 min read

Harvey
harvey.ai ↗

Harvey is the leading legal AI platform — used by Allen & Overy, PwC, and many BigLaw firms for contract analysis, document drafting, and legal research. Series E, $5B+ valuation. The interview emphasizes vertical-AI engineering, retrieval over very large legal document corpora, and the regulated-industry care of working with privileged data.

Process

Recruiter screen → 60-minute coding (Python or TypeScript) → onsite virtual: 2 coding, 1 system design (LLM-flavored), 1 craft deep-dive, 1 behavioral. Cycle: 3–5 weeks.

What they actually ask

- Design a RAG pipeline over millions of legal documents per customer

- Design a contract-analysis system that compares thousands of provisions

- Design an evaluation harness for legal-correctness (high-stakes domain)

- Coding: medium DSA, often with NLP or pipeline framing

- Behavioral: ownership, customer empathy for lawyers, regulated-industry care

Levels and comp (2026)

- SE: $185K–$255K total (cash + late-stage equity)

- Senior SE: $270K–$370K total

- Staff: $385K–$535K total

- Principal: $545K–$740K total

Prep priorities

- Be fluent in Python (ML/AI services) and TypeScript / Node.js (product surface)

- Understand RAG patterns deeply (chunking, embeddings, reranking, structured extraction)

- Brush up on legal data formats, privilege/confidentiality concerns, and audit-trail patterns

Frequently Asked Questions

Is Harvey remote-friendly?

Hubs in San Francisco (HQ) and NYC. Most engineering roles fully remote within US.

How does Harvey compare to Casetext or EvenUp?

Casetext was acquired by Thomson Reuters and serves a broader market. EvenUp focuses on personal injury / plaintiff side. Harvey is BigLaw-focused premium. Comp at Harvey is competitive at top of late-stage AI company bands.

What is the engineering culture?

Senior-heavy hires, customer-obsessed (BigLaw partners are demanding), fast-shipping. Calmer than frontier-lab pace; more product-engineering balance.
